title: Write a Cross-Account Trust Policy
description:
## Let a CI Account Assume a Role

Cross-account access is done with role assumption: the target account defines a role whose **trust policy** allows a trusted account to call **`sts:AssumeRole`** — no long-lived keys shared.

**Your task:** write a trust policy at **`/root/trust.json`** that allows the AWS account **`111122223333`** to assume the role (Principal `AWS`, action `sts:AssumeRole`, Effect Allow).

> 💡 The trust (assume-role) policy uses `"Principal": { "AWS": "arn:aws:iam::111122223333:root" }` and `"Action": "sts:AssumeRole"`.

hints.title: Trust policy shape
hints.body: Allow the trusted account's root principal to call sts:AssumeRole.

validation.description: Checks for an Allow statement with an AWS principal and sts:AssumeRole.

commands.description: Allows sts:AssumeRole for an AWS principal
commands.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/root/trust.json')); s=d['Statement']; s=s if isinstance(s,list) else [s]; ok=any(st.get('Effect')=='Allow' and 'sts:AssumeRole' in (st['Action'] if isinstance(st['Action'],list) else [st['Action']]) and 'AWS' in (st.get('Principal') or {}) for st in s); print('ok' if ok else 'no')"
